content_html:
Foo Yun Chee, reporting for Reuters back on March 23:

Google, Amazon, Apple and Samsung‚Äôs smart TVs and virtual assistants should fall under the EU‚Äôs toughest tech rules because of their growing market power, the world‚Äôs largest broadcasters told EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera on Monday.

The call by the Association of Commercial Television and Video on Demand Services in Europe (ACT) whose members include Canal+, RTL, Mediaset, ITV, Paramount+, NBCUniversal, Walt Disney, Warner Bros Discovery, Sky and TF1 Groupe underscores the battle between ‚Äãbroadcasters and Big Tech for market share in a lucrative industry.

Android TV, which increased its market share from 16% to ‚Äã23% from 2019 to 2024, Amazon Fire OS whose market share rose from 5% to 12% in the same period and Samsung‚Äôs Tizen OS with its 24% market share should be designated as gatekeepers under the EU‚Äôs ‚ÄãDigital Markets Act, the broadcasters said, citing data from a 2025 market study.

Apple is mentioned only in the context of voice assistants, not Apple TV 4K:

The broadcasters also voiced concerns about virtual assistants, the most ‚Äãwell known of which are Amazon‚Äôs ‚ÄãAlexa and Apple‚Äôs Siri, while OpenAI entered the field last year with a beta feature called Tasks for its AI chatbot ChatGPT. The European Commission has yet to label any virtual assistants as gatekeepers under the DMA. [...]

They urged Ribera to subject smart TVs and virtual assistants to the DMA on the basis of qualitative criteria even if they do not meet the quantitative benchmarks which ‚Äãare more than 45 million monthly active users and 75 billion euros ($87 ‚Äãbillion) in market capitalisation.

I found this story only after posting the previous item, trying to see if there were any DMA-related actions that I‚Äôd missed under Ribera‚Äôs leadership. I didn‚Äôt find much. And this Reuters story only says the broadcasters sent Ribera a letter asking her to go after smart TV platforms and voice assistants‚Äâ‚Äî‚Äâthere‚Äôs no suggestion that Ribera intends to do so.

title: Broadcasters Urge EU to Use the DMA to Go After Smart TV Platforms, None of Which Are From European Companies
